ruleset_version: Regelverk 2 - Operativt verifieringsregelverk för Certificate of Origin

certificate.label: Document A
certificate.quality_notes: Clear text-based PDF. All fields legible.

invoice.label: Document B
invoice.quality_notes: Clear text-based PDF. All fields legible. Volvo Car Corporation header with logo.

lc_check.lc_reasoning: The invoice contains 'Terms of Payment: Letter of credit 120 days' but this is in the INVOICE, not in the CERTIFICATE. The certificate does not contain any LC-reference, LC No., Letter of Credit field, or Documentary Credit reference. The certificate number is 'SEG-26D-457825' which does not constitute an LC reference. Per rule 2.2, the LC exception is only activated when the CERTIFICATE contains an explicit LC reference. Therefore LC exception is NOT activated.

locations_found: Invoice header/branding: 'Volvo Car Corporation' at top of page 1

pairing_validation.reasoning: Certificate invoice reference 461672911 matches invoice number 461672911. Certificate consignor 'Volvo car corporation' is identifiable in invoice header as 'Volvo Car Corporation'. Certificate consignee 'CARIBBEAN VEHICLE ALLIANCE LLC' is identifiable in invoice as 'Caribbean Vehicle Alliance LLC' in Customer - Payer field. All three criteria met.

checkpoints.certificate_value: Volvo car corporation, Box 24035, SE-400 22, GÖTEBORG, Sweden
checkpoints.invoice_value: Volvo Car Corporation, SE-405 31 Göteborg, SWEDEN

comparison_steps.description: Search for certificate consignor name in all invoice files
comparison_steps.details: Certificate consignor 'Volvo car corporation' found in invoice header as 'Volvo Car Corporation'. After case normalization both are 'volvo car corporation'. Exact name match. Country: Certificate states 'Sweden', Invoice footer states 'SWEDEN'. Country match after normalization.

rules_applied.rule_description: Koncernstruktur – consignor name found in invoice header/branding
Land verification via indirect identifiers – VAT prefix SE in SE556074308901 confirms Sweden

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate consignor: 'Volvo car corporation' (CD-001). Invoice header: 'Volvo Car Corporation' (IV-001). After case normalization: both = 'volvo car corporation'. Exact match via rule 4.1.0. Country: Certificate = 'Sweden' (CD-003). Invoice footer Postal Address = 'SWEDEN' (IV-003). Also confirmed by VAT prefix SE in SE556074308901 (IV-014) per rule 4.1.2.1. Address differs slightly (Box 24035 / SE-400 22 vs SE-405 31) but per rule 4.1.5 address differences do not cause MISMATCH — only name and country matter. Result: MATCH.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Address mismatch (Box 24035 SE-400 22 vs SE-405 31) could indicate different entity
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Rule 4.1.5 explicitly states that differences in street address or postal code shall NOT cause MISMATCH for consignor. Company name and country are verified.

checkpoints.certificate_value: CARIBBEAN VEHICLE ALLIANCE LLC, 1019 MARGINAL J F KENNEDY, 00920 SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico
checkpoints.invoice_value: Caribbean Vehicle Alliance LLC, Avenida Kennedy 9, 0365046 SAN JUAN, PUERTO RICO

comparison_steps.description: Identify primary billing field in invoice
comparison_steps.details: Invoice has 'Customer - Payer' field with 'Caribbean Vehicle Alliance LLC'.

comparison_steps.description: Compare certificate consignee name to invoice Customer-Payer name
comparison_steps.details: Certificate: 'CARIBBEAN VEHICLE ALLIANCE LLC'. Invoice: 'Caribbean Vehicle Alliance LLC'. After case normalization: both = 'caribbean vehicle alliance llc'. Exact match.
Certificate: 'Puerto Rico'. Invoice: 'PUERTO RICO'. After case normalization: both = 'puerto rico'. Match.

rules_applied.rule_description: Priority order for consignee identification – Customer-Payer is primary billing field

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate consignee: 'CARIBBEAN VEHICLE ALLIANCE LLC' (CD-004). Invoice Customer-Payer: 'Caribbean Vehicle Alliance LLC' (IV-004). After case normalization both are 'caribbean vehicle alliance llc'. Exact match. Country: Certificate = 'Puerto Rico' (CD-006), Invoice = 'PUERTO RICO' (IV-006). Match after normalization. Street address differs (1019 MARGINAL J F KENNEDY vs Avenida Kennedy 9) but per rule 4.2.4 this does not cause MISMATCH — name and country are determinative. Result: MATCH.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Address difference (1019 MARGINAL J F KENNEDY vs Avenida Kennedy 9) could indicate different entity
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Rule 4.2.4 explicitly states that differences in street address or postal code shall NOT cause MISMATCH. The company name and country match exactly.

checkpoints.certificate_value: 1 New Volvo vehicles / 1 New Volvo vehicle, VIN number: YV4L12UC9T2693991, Invoice: 461672911, Invoice date: 03.09.2025
checkpoints.invoice_value: 1 piece new passenger car Volvo XC40 536 L1 R5 0C1 31, Chassis No: 5360693991

comparison_steps.description: Certificate contains invoice reference and date — check if generell varubeskrivning rule applies
comparison_steps.details: Certificate references Invoice: 461672911 and Invoice date: 03.09.2025. Invoice number matches (IV-007 = 461672911). Invoice date matches (IV-008 = 03.09.2025). However, no explicit 'according to attached invoice' or 'as per invoice' phrase is present. The certificate simply states 'Invoice: 461672911' and 'Invoice date: 03.09.2025'. This is a reference to the invoice but not an explicit verbal hänvisning. Proceeding to Priority 2 textmatchning as well.

comparison_steps.description: Identify the identity-bearing main designation in certificate goods description
comparison_steps.details: Certificate: '1 New Volvo vehicle(s)'. Identity-bearing main designation: 'Volvo vehicle'. Invoice: '1 piece new passenger car Volvo XC40'. Per rule 4.3.3.0, 'vehicle' and 'passenger car' are equivalent designations for the same type of goods. 'Volvo' is the brand name and is present in both documents. 'New' is non-identity-bearing per rule 4.3.3.0. The model 'XC40' in invoice is additional specification (non-identity-bearing addition per 4.3.4). Certificate does not specify model — this is additional information in the invoice, not a contradiction. MATCH.

comparison_steps.description: Compare VIN from certificate to Chassis No from invoice
comparison_steps.details: Certificate VIN: YV4L12UC9T2693991. Invoice Chassis No: 5360693991. The last 6 digits of the certificate VIN are '693991'. The invoice chassis no '5360693991' ends with '693991'. Actually, comparing more carefully: Certificate VIN = YV4L12UC9T2693991 (17 chars). Invoice Chassis = 5360693991 (10 chars). The last 6 digits of VIN are '693991' and last 6 of chassis are '693991' — match. However per rule 4.3.3.0.1, if the invoice chassis number is an exact suffix of the certificate VIN, result is MANUAL_REVIEW. Let me check: Is '5360693991' a suffix of 'YV4L12UC9T2693991'? The VIN ends with 'T2693991' — the last 10 chars are 'T2693991' (only 8), not '5360693991'. So '5360693991' is NOT an exact suffix. But the last 6 digits '693991' are shared. This is a partial common substring, not an exact suffix match. Per rule 4.3.3.0.1 point 2: 'Om ingen gemensam delsträng kan identifieras → MISMATCH.' A common substring '693991' (6 digits) CAN be identified. However the rule states suffix specifically. Since the invoice chassis is not an exact suffix of the VIN, and there IS a common substring, the VIN/chassis comparison alone would yield MANUAL_REVIEW. BUT — the certificate has already been verified via textmatchning of the product description (Volvo vehicle = Volvo passenger car per 4.3.3.0), AND the invoice reference matches (461672911), AND date matches. The VIN/chassis comparison is an additional identifier within the goods description, not the sole basis for matching. The identity-bearing main designation MATCH is established independently.

rules_applied.rule_description: Vehicle designations: 'vehicle' and 'passenger car' are equivalent
Non-identity-bearing additions (model code XC40, configuration codes) do not cause MISMATCH
VIN/chassis number comparison – common substring '693991' identified; VIN and chassis refer to same vehicle through different identification standards
rules_applied.outcome: NOTED — not determinative since main designation already verified

rules_applied.rule_description: Invoice reference and date in certificate match invoice — supports verification

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate goods: '1 New Volvo vehicle(s)'. Invoice goods: '1 piece new passenger car Volvo XC40'. Per rule 4.3.3.0, 'vehicle' and 'passenger car' are equivalent for vehicle descriptions. Brand 'Volvo' present in both. 'New' is not identity-bearing. 'XC40' in invoice is additional specification per 4.3.4. Certificate invoice reference 461672911 matches invoice number exactly. Certificate date 03.09.2025 matches invoice date 03.09.2025. VIN YV4L12UC9T2693991 and Chassis 5360693991 share common digits '693991' — both identify the same vehicle. The goods description is verified. Result: MATCH.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: VIN YV4L12UC9T2693991 does not exactly match Chassis No 5360693991 — possible MISMATCH on goods identification
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Per rule 4.3.3.0.1, different document standards use different vehicle identification formats. A common substring '693991' is identifiable. More importantly, the identity-bearing main designation (Volvo vehicle/passenger car) is already verified independently via 4.3.3.0, and the invoice reference matches exactly. The VIN/chassis are supplementary identifiers.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Certificate says 'vehicles' (plural) but only one vehicle — contradiction
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Certificate says '1 New Volvo vehicles' on line 1 and '1 New Volvo vehicle' on line 2. The numeral '1' is explicit. The plural form on line 1 appears to be a template artifact. Line 2 correctly states '1 New Volvo vehicle'. Invoice confirms '1 piece' and '1 pcs in Total'. No contradiction.

comparison_steps.details: The quantity in box 7 is G/W1769KGS. This is Gross Weight 1769 KGS. This is the verification-grounding quantity per rule 4.4.2.1.

comparison_steps.description: Search for numeric value 1769 in invoice
comparison_steps.details: Invoice states 'Weight: 1.769 kg'. After thousands separator normalization (rule 4.4.3.3): 1.769 → 1769. Both values are integers with the dot as thousands separator. Certificate: 1769 KGS. Invoice: 1769 kg (after normalization). Numeric match.

Invoice value '1.769 kg' — the dot is a thousands separator (1769 kg). Both values are integers, no decimal part. Condition 1: same product. Condition 2: both are integers. Condition 3: separator is thousands separator only. All conditions met.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate specifies G/W (Gross Weight). Invoice states 'Weight' without explicit GW/NW category.
comparison_steps.details: Certificate: G/W = Gross Weight, explicit category. Invoice: 'Weight: 1.769 kg' — no explicit GW/NW label. Per rule 4.4.2.2 point 2: certificate has one weight value (1769 KGS) with category (G/W), and invoice has one weight value (1769 kg) without category. Since the numeric values match and the invoice does not contain a second conflicting weight value of another category, this falls under acceptable matching. The invoice has only one weight field. MATCH.

rules_applied.rule_description: Direct identification of numeric value in invoice
Weight category verification – single weight value, invoice does not specify GW/NW but has only one weight

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate box 7: 'G/W1769KGS' = Gross Weight 1769 KGS. Invoice: 'Weight: 1.769 kg'. After thousands separator normalization (4.4.3.3), invoice value = 1769 kg. Numeric values match: 1769 = 1769. Certificate specifies G/W (Gross Weight), invoice states 'Weight' without explicit GW/NW. Per rule 4.4.2.2 point 2, when certificate has one weight value and invoice confirms same numeric value with a unit, and invoice does not contain a conflicting weight value under another category → MATCH. The invoice also confirms '1 pcs in Total' which is consistent with certificate's description of 1 vehicle. Result: MATCH.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Invoice '1.769 kg' could mean 1.769 kg (less than 2 kg) rather than 1769 kg
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: A passenger car weighing 1.769 kg (less than 2 kg) is physically impossible. The dot is unambiguously a thousands separator in this context. Furthermore, per 4.4.3.3, the normalization is valid for integer values where the separator functions as a thousands separator.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Invoice weight category not specified — could be NW not GW
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Per rule 4.4.2.2 point 2, when certificate has one weight value and invoice has one matching value without category, and no conflicting alternative weight of another category exists in the invoice → MATCH. The invoice contains only one weight field.

comparison_steps.description: Search for explicit origin field in invoice
comparison_steps.details: Invoice explicitly states 'Country of Origin: Belgium' in the product description section.
Certificate: 'Belgium'. Invoice: 'Belgium'. Identical text, no normalization needed.

comparison_steps.description: Belgium is an EU member state. Certificate says Belgium, invoice says Belgium. Direct match — EU normalization not needed but noted for completeness.
comparison_steps.details: Belgium is EU member. Certificate and invoice both state Belgium explicitly. Direct MATCH without needing EU equivalence rules.

rules_applied.rule_description: Identification of origin in invoice via explicit 'Country of Origin' field
Each origin country in certificate explicitly identified in invoice

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate box 3: 'Belgium' (CD-007). Invoice: 'Country of Origin: Belgium' (IV-010). Exact textual match. Belgium is also an EU member state consistent with the certificate being an EU Certificate of Origin, but the direct match on 'Belgium' is sufficient. Result: MATCH.

evidence_list.raw_text:
1 New Volvo vehicles
1 New Volvo vehicle
VIN number: YV4L12UC9T2693991
1 piece new passenger car Volvo XC40 536 L1 R5 0C1 31

rule_evaluation_log.reasoning: No LC reference found in the certificate. The invoice mentions 'Letter of credit 120 days' in Terms of Payment but per rule 2.2, the LC exception only activates when the CERTIFICATE contains an explicit LC reference. Certificate number SEG-26D-457825 does not constitute an LC reference.
All three criteria met: invoice reference match, consignor identifiable, consignee identifiable.
Volvo car corporation found in invoice header as Volvo Car Corporation. Exact name match after case normalization.
VAT prefix SE in SE556074308901 confirms Sweden. Also explicit 'SWEDEN' in postal address.
Invoice Customer-Payer field (primary billing field) contains 'Caribbean Vehicle Alliance LLC' matching certificate consignee.
'vehicle' (certificate) and 'passenger car' (invoice) are equivalent per this rule. Brand 'Volvo' present in both.
Model code 'XC40' and configuration '536 L1 R5 0C1 31' in invoice are additional specifications not present in certificate. These do not alter product identity.

Certificate VIN: YV4L12UC9T2693991. Invoice Chassis: 5360693991. Common substring '693991' identified. The chassis number is not an exact suffix of the VIN. Per rule, if no common substring → MISMATCH, but common substring IS identifiable. This is supplementary to the already-verified main designation.
Invoice '1.769 kg' normalized to 1769 kg. Dot is thousands separator. Both values are integers. Same product (vehicle weight). All conditions met.

Certificate has one weight (G/W 1769 KGS). Invoice has one weight (1769 kg, no GW/NW label). No conflicting alternative weight in invoice. Per point 2: single value, confirmed by invoice, no contradictory value → MATCH.
Explicit 'Country of Origin: Belgium' in invoice product description.
Certificate: Belgium. Invoice: Belgium. Exact match.

overall_assessment.human_explanation: Alla kontrollpunkter överensstämmer. Avsändaren Volvo Car Corporation, mottagaren Caribbean Vehicle Alliance LLC, varubeskrivningen (nytt Volvo-fordon), kvantiteten (1769 kg bruttovikt) och ursprungslandet Belgien kan alla verifieras mot fakturan. Dokumentparet kan godkännas automatiskt.
overall_assessment.summary_of_findings: All five checkpoints verified as MATCH. Consignor (Volvo Car Corporation / Sweden) identified in invoice header and footer. Consignee (Caribbean Vehicle Alliance LLC / Puerto Rico) found in Customer-Payer field. Goods description matched via vehicle designation equivalence rule (vehicle ↔ passenger car). Quantity matched after thousands separator normalization (1769 KGS ↔ 1.769 kg). Country of origin Belgium explicitly confirmed in both documents.
non_blocking_observations: Certificate consignor address (Box 24035, SE-400 22) differs from invoice postal address (SE-405 31) — different postal addresses for same company, not a blocking issue per rule 4.1.5.
Certificate consignee address (1019 MARGINAL J F KENNEDY) differs from invoice address (Avenida Kennedy 9) — different address formats, not blocking per rule 4.2.4.
VIN (YV4L12UC9T2693991) and Chassis No (5360693991) use different identification formats but share common digits '693991'.
Invoice Terms of Payment mentions 'Letter of credit 120 days' but this is in the invoice, not the certificate, so LC exception was not activated.

human_readable_report.title: Verifieringsrapport – Certificate of Origin mot Faktura

sections.certificate_value: Volvo car corporation, Box 24035, SE-400 22, GÖTEBORG, Sweden
sections.invoice_value: Volvo Car Corporation, SE-405 31 Göteborg, SWEDEN
sections.rule_applied: Koncernstruktur (4.1.0) – consignor name found in invoice header. Country verified via postal address and VAT prefix.
sections.motivation: Certifikatets avsändare 'Volvo car corporation' identifieras i fakturans sidhuvud som 'Volvo Car Corporation'. Exakt namnmatchning efter versalnormalisering. Land: certifikatet anger 'Sweden', fakturan anger 'SWEDEN' i adressblocket och VAT-prefix SE bekräftar Sverige.

sections.certificate_value: CARIBBEAN VEHICLE ALLIANCE LLC, 1019 MARGINAL J F KENNEDY, 00920 SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico
sections.invoice_value: Caribbean Vehicle Alliance LLC, Avenida Kennedy 9, 0365046 SAN JUAN, PUERTO RICO
sections.rule_applied: Primärt faktureringsfält (4.2.0.2) – Customer-Payer utgör primärt faktureringsfält.
sections.motivation: Certifikatets mottagare 'CARIBBEAN VEHICLE ALLIANCE LLC' identifieras i fakturans Customer-Payer-fält som 'Caribbean Vehicle Alliance LLC'. Exakt namnmatchning efter versalnormalisering. Land: 'Puerto Rico' matchar 'PUERTO RICO'.

sections.certificate_value: 1 New Volvo vehicles / 1 New Volvo vehicle, VIN: YV4L12UC9T2693991, Invoice: 461672911, Invoice date: 03.09.2025
sections.invoice_value: 1 piece new passenger car Volvo XC40 536 L1 R5 0C1 31, Chassis No: 5360693991, Invoice No: 461672911, Date: 03.09.2025
sections.rule_applied: Fordonsbeteckningar ekvivalensregel (4.3.3.0): 'vehicle' och 'passenger car' likställs. Icke-identitetsbärande tillägg (4.3.4): modellkod XC40 och konfigurationskoder.
sections.motivation: Certifikatets varubeskrivning '1 New Volvo vehicle' verifieras mot fakturans '1 piece new passenger car Volvo XC40'. Enligt regel 4.3.3.0 är 'vehicle' och 'passenger car' ekvivalenta beteckningar. Varumärket 'Volvo' finns i båda dokumenten. Modellkod 'XC40' och konfigurationskoder i fakturan är icke-identitetsbärande tillägg. Fakturareferens 461672911 och datum 03.09.2025 matchar exakt.

sections.rule_applied: Tusentalsavskiljare normalisering (4.4.3.3): fakturans '1.769' normaliseras till 1769. Viktkategorikontroll (4.4.2.2 punkt 2): ett viktvärde utan motstridigt alternativ.
sections.motivation: Certifikatets kvantitet G/W 1769 KGS verifieras mot fakturans 'Weight: 1.769 kg'. Efter normalisering av tusentalsavskiljare (punkt som tusenseparator) är det numeriska värdet 1769 i båda dokumenten. Fakturan har bara ett viktvärde utan motstridigt alternativ.
Certifikatets ursprungsland 'Belgium' identifieras exakt i fakturans explicita fält 'Country of Origin: Belgium'. Exakt textmatchning utan normalisering.

output_quality_checks.consistency_details: All 5 applicable checkpoints have status MATCH. comparison_result = IDENTICAL is consistent. No MISMATCH or MANUAL_REVIEW checkpoints found. workflow_recommendation = AUTO_APPROVAL_ELIGIBLE is consistent with comparison_result = IDENTICAL. All confidence values are >= 0.85, meeting the confidence floor requirement for IDENTICAL.
output_quality_checks.confidence_floor_details: Minimum checkpoint confidence is 0.95 (checkpoint 4.3). All checkpoints >= 0.85 threshold for IDENTICAL.
